sourceText: He endured rigorous training to become a printer amidst childhood poverty and his father's opposition, ran away from home due to conflict with his brother, faced the threat of business failure, experienced prolonged separations from his family due to political activities, and navigated the uncertainties of the Revolutionary War.

He overcame hardships through continuous self-improvement and diligence, acquired knowledge through self-study, successfully led various businesses, and contributed to community development by organizing civic groups. His exceptional diplomacy and foresight played a crucial role in nation-building.
Emphasizes the importance of using time efficiently and not wasting it.
It is better to learn from failure than from success.
Suggests that failure provides opportunities for growth and can offer deeper lessons than success.
He that will not apply himself shall not be forward.
Highlights that success depends on one's own effort and will.
Accusations of corruption and imprisonment during his political career.
Achieved immortality through scholarly works and philosophical ideas.
Knowledge is the core driving force that empowers individuals and leads to societal progress.
